_description: 5 discovery questions with known-correct answers derived from Olist FK ground truth. Agent answers are scored against these at eval time.

table_set.definition: Compare the set of tables the agent identified as required against tables_required. Precision = |agent ∩ expected| / |agent|. Recall = |agent ∩ expected| / |expected|. Order does not matter.

join_path.definition: Each expected join is a directed edge (from_table, join_column, to_table). An agent edge matches if from_table, the join column(s), and to_table all match. Precision = matched_agent_edges / total_agent_edges. Recall = matched_agent_edges / total_expected_edges.
join_path.notes: For Q4, where from_column ≠ to_column, both column names must match for the edge to count as correct.

questions.question: Which table tells me who placed a given order?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Join olist_orders_dataset to olist_customers_dataset on customer_id to get the customer who placed an order.
questions.eval_notes: Simplest possible single-hop. Tests whether agent finds the most direct FK.

questions.question: I want to find the product category in English and seller city for every order item. Which tables do I need and how do I join them?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Start from olist_order_items_dataset. Join to olist_products_dataset on product_id for category (in Portuguese), then join to product_category_name_translation on product_category_name for English category. Separately join to olist_sellers_dataset on seller_id for seller city.
questions.eval_notes: Tests multi-hop and a non-obvious bridge table (the translation table).

questions.question: How do I get the review score for each order, along with what the customer paid?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Start from olist_orders_dataset and join both olist_order_reviews_dataset and olist_order_payments_dataset on order_id. These are two parallel one-to-many joins from the central orders table.
questions.eval_notes: Tests detection of two parallel fact satellites hanging off the same FK.

questions.question: Can I map a customer's delivery address to geographic coordinates?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Join olist_customers_dataset to olist_geolocation_dataset on customer_zip_code_prefix = geolocation_zip_code_prefix. Note: this is a many-to-many relationship since each zip prefix maps to multiple lat/lng coordinates.
questions.eval_notes: Tests detection of a cross-column-name join (column names differ) and a many-to-many cardinality.

questions.question: I want a single query showing order date, customer city, product category (in English), seller state, review score, and payment type. What is the full join path?
questions.expected_answer_summary: olist_orders is the hub. Fan out to: olist_customers (customer_id), olist_order_items (order_id), olist_order_reviews (order_id), olist_order_payments (order_id). From order_items fan out to olist_products (product_id) → product_category_name_translation (category name), and olist_sellers (seller_id).
questions.eval_notes: The hardest test — requires traversing all 8 tables via the full star schema. High edge count means partial credit is expected; scoring follows the same rubric as Q1–Q4.
